Africa travel listings, destination insight, stays, restaurants, and partner booking paths for better-planned journeys. Compare where to go, where to stay, and where to eat before you book.

Image slot Home Hero Northern Africa

East Africa

Safari plains, primate forests, highland culture, and Indian Ocean extensions.

Compare Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Rwanda, and Ethiopia through the experiences they do best, from gorilla trekking and migration safaris to coast and heritage routes.

Explore Regions Browse Listings

Start with fit

Africa is not one travel style. It is many strong choices.

Some travelers come for wildlife and primates, others for food, coast, history, design, desert silence, family-friendly resorts, or a meaningful heritage journey. The best trip starts by matching the region, country, attraction, stay, and dining scene to the kind of experience you actually want.

How Trek Africa Guide works

  • Begin with the part of Africa that matches your travel style: safari, coast, culture, desert, food, heritage, or a mix of several.
  • Open a destination guide to see the attractions, stays, restaurants, and operators that make sense together.
  • Compare the practical details first, then continue to an external booking partner when a listing is worth a closer look.

Featured Regions

Four useful starting points for a smarter Africa trip.

Use the regions to compare travel moods first, then narrow into destination countries, attractions, nearby stays, restaurants, and booking paths.

Image slot Region East Africa
5 destination countries

East Africa

East Africa remains one of the continent’s highest-demand regions for safari, gorilla trekking, coast add-ons, and premium conservation travel. Kenya and Tanzania lead the classic plains circuits, while Uganda and Rwanda anchor primate-led journeys.
Image slot Region West Africa
5 destination countries

West Africa

West Africa is driven by heritage journeys, winter-sun escapes, coastal cities, and community-grounded experiences. Ghana and Senegal are the most polished starting points, while Benin, Sierra Leone, and Cabo Verde broaden the offer considerably.
Image slot Region Southern Africa
5 destination countries

Southern Africa

Southern Africa is one of the continent’s most versatile travel regions. South Africa acts as the gateway, Botswana and Namibia deliver high-value wilderness and desert landscapes, and Zimbabwe and Zambia deepen the safari-and-Zambezi story.
Image slot Region Northern Africa
4 destination countries

Northern Africa

Northern Africa is Africa’s volume engine for tourism, driven by Morocco, Egypt, Tunisia, and a growing wave of renewed interest in Algeria. Travelers come for cities, heritage, desert camps, Mediterranean coastlines, and food-led travel.

Featured Attractions

Attractions that can become the anchor of the journey.

Open each listing for a fuller sense of the place: why it matters, how to get there, when to go, where to stay nearby, where to eat, and what to check before booking.

Image slot Attraction Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Uganda

Bwindi Impenetrable National Park

Mountain gorilla trekking in one of Africa’s most powerful forest landscapes.

★ 4.9 (842) From $700
Southwestern Uganda
View Details
Image slot Attraction Maasai Mara

Kenya

Maasai Mara

Kenya’s classic safari icon for migration drama, predators, and broad savannah horizons.

★ 4.9 (1,384) From $95
Southwestern Kenya
View Details
Image slot Attraction Serengeti National Park

Tanzania

Serengeti National Park

Vast migration landscapes, apex predators, and one of Africa’s strongest safari names.

★ 4.9 (1,196) From $110
Northern Tanzania
View Details
Image slot Attraction Cape Town

South Africa

Cape Town

A design-led city with ocean views, wine access, and easy day trips.

★ 4.8 (1,675) From $20
Western Cape
View Details
Image slot Attraction Victoria Falls

Zimbabwe

Victoria Falls

The classic waterfall gateway for rainforest walks, river activities, and onward safari routing.

★ 4.7 (882) From $28
Zimbabwe
View Details
Image slot Attraction Marrakech And Atlas

Morocco

Marrakech & Atlas Gateway

A flexible route of medina culture, gardens, riads, and day trips into the Atlas foothills.

★ 4.7 (1,222) From $16
Morocco
View Details
View more attractions

Featured Stays

Stays chosen for how they support the route.

Compare lodges, riads, camps, beach resorts, and city hotels by the attraction or destination experience they make easier.

Image slot Stay Sanctuary Gorilla Forest Camp

Uganda

Sanctuary Gorilla Forest Camp

A long-established forest camp positioned inside the Bwindi area for early gorilla-trekking starts and a quieter rainforest stay.

★ 4.8 (318) From $800 / night
Forest safari camp Bwindi Impenetrable National Park
View Details
Image slot Stay Paraa Safari Lodge

Uganda

Paraa Safari Lodge

A practical Nile-side lodge for travelers combining boat safaris, falls visits, and northern-bank game drives.

★ 4.5 (521) From $220 / night
Safari lodge Murchison Falls National Park
View Details
Image slot Stay Governors Camp

Kenya

Governors' Camp

A historic tented camp in a strong game-viewing zone, often used by travelers who want classic Mara atmosphere without losing comfort.

★ 4.7 (684) From $620 / person
Safari tented camp Maasai Mara
View Details
Image slot Stay Ol Tukai Lodge Amboseli

Kenya

Ol Tukai Lodge Amboseli

A well-known Amboseli base for elephant viewing and Kilimanjaro-facing safari days.

★ 4.6 (477) From $300 / night
Safari lodge Amboseli National Park
View Details
View more accommodations

Featured Restaurants

Restaurants and lodge dining that make the place feel complete.

Dining sits alongside attractions and stays because meals often shape the rhythm of a day as much as transport and sightseeing do.

Image slot Restaurant Sanctuary Gorilla Forest Camp Dining

Uganda

Sanctuary Gorilla Forest Camp Dining

A realistic dining option for Bwindi because most visitors eat at their lodge before and after trekking rather than browsing standalone restaurants.

★ 4.5 (126) $$$
Lodge dining Bwindi Impenetrable National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Paraa Safari Lodge Dining

Uganda

Paraa Safari Lodge Dining

Murchison dining is usually lodge-based because game drives and boat departures shape the day. Paraa is practical for travelers staying near the river.

★ 4.3 (218) $$
Lodge dining Murchison Falls National Park
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Governors Camp Dining

Kenya

Governors' Camp Dining

The Mara is not a city restaurant destination; dining is usually tied to camp quality, guide timing, and whether bush meals are included.

★ 4.6 (311) $$$
Safari camp dining Maasai Mara
View Details
Image slot Restaurant Ol Tukai Lodge Dining

Kenya

Ol Tukai Lodge Dining

Ol Tukai dining works because visitors are usually inside the park for early and late game drives, making lodge meals more realistic than off-site restaurant runs.

★ 4.4 (205) $$
Safari lodge dining Amboseli National Park
View Details
View more restaurants
